response.overview: Arthur J. Bressan, Jr.'s pioneering gay adult drama, Passing Strangers tells the story of two gay men in San Francisco who meet via a newspaper ad and fall in love. The stunning Robert Adams stars as Robert, the 18 year-old high school senior who is the object of affection for the film's 28-year old protagonist Tom (Robert Carnagey). Tom's curly locks and luscious good looks seem to make him irresistible to all - as he spends his time cruising Polk Street, going to the baths and the bars - but when Robert responds to his personal ad in the gay paper, Tom finds himself falling in love. It offers an earnest and deeply engaging gay love story at its core. —Jenni Olson
response.title: Passing Strangers
